title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
summary: This talk addresses a critical failure mode in web-accessing agents: they may claim to have searched the web while actually falling back to stale training data due to blocking, CAPTCHAs, or fake pages. The speaker demonstrates the difference between blocked and unblocked agent runs and explains the technical barriers including anti-bot systems, JavaScript rendering, and CAPTCHA handling.
writeup: This AI Engineer talk from Rafael Levi of Bright Data examines a failure mode in web-accessing agents: an agent can report that it searched the web while actually returning stale training data because it was blocked by anti-bot systems, CAPTCHAs, or fake pages. Levi contrasts blocked and unblocked agent runs to show the gap, and walks through the technical barriers that cause it, including bot detection, JavaScript rendering, and CAPTCHA handling. The relevance is reliability: agents that silently fall back undermine trust, so the discussion ties into hallucination detection and citations as ways to verify that retrieval actually happened.
